us-politics.headline: Two US Warplanes Down Over Iran, Crew Member Missing
us-politics.body:
Two US warplanes were shot down over Iran, with a search underway for at least one missing crew member. The incident marks a sharp escalation in what has become an active US military engagement in the region. Public polling shows roughly half of Americans expect Middle East stability to worsen.

The political fallout is immediate. Rep. Nancy Mace broke with the administration, saying she is "concerned about escalation" and opposes deploying ground troops without congressional approval. A Democrat slammed prediction market Polymarket for taking bets on the missing pilot's fate. NPR analysis notes Trump's wartime rhetoric departs significantly from how past presidents have communicated during military operations - a distinction observers say carries real consequences for public trust and allied coordination.

sources.title: Two U.S. warplanes shot down; search ongoing in Iran for 1 missing crew member
Mace ‘concerned about escalation’ in Iran war
Democrat rips Polymarket for taking bets on fate of US pilot shot down over Iran
How Trump's wartime rhetoric differs from past presidents and why that matters
About half expect worsening stability in Middle East: Survey

us-politics.headline: Pentagon Tensions Rise as Hegseth Ousts Respected Army Chief
us-politics.body:
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth removed the Army chief of staff, drawing an unusual wave of praise for the ousted general from Republican lawmakers - a rare public split between the GOP and the Pentagon's civilian leadership. The episode adds to what multiple outlets describe as a rocky stretch for the administration at home and abroad.

Separately, Sen. Thom Tillis drew a firm line on the attorney general nominee, saying any candidate who refuses to affirm the January 6th Capitol attack was a riot will lose his vote. Todd Blanche, now heading the Justice Department, faces scrutiny over his dual role as Trump's former personal defense attorney. The convergence of military and legal tensions is testing Republican unity heading into a consequential confirmation period.

sources.title: Tillis sets Jan. 6 red line on attorney general nominee
GOP praise pours in for Army chief of staff ousted by Hegseth
President Trump's rocky week at home and abroad
Todd Blanche takes on DOJ’s top job and the burden of Trump’s expectations

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Targets TSA, Mail Voting, and DC Skyline
us-politics.body:
The White House budget proposal cuts TSA funding and mandates privatization at smaller airports, while simultaneously requesting $10 billion for Washington DC construction and beautification projects and $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a federal prison. Democrats called the overall package "bleak and unacceptable." Vice President Vance was named a "fraud czar" tasked with investigating benefit payments in Democratic-led states.

On voting, Democratic-led states filed suit after Trump signed an executive order tightening mail-in ballot rules, arguing it exceeds presidential authority. Senate Democrats separately pressed Treasury over the decision to add Trump's name to US currency. The legal and political battles over both moves are expected to intensify quickly.

sources.title: Senate Democrats press Treasury on decision to add Trump name to currency
Trump proposes $10B fund for D.C. construction, beautification projects
Democratic-led states sue over Trump mail-in ballot order
Trump budget would cut TSA funding, require privatization at small airports
Vance tapped as ‘fraud czar’ as Trump targets blue states over taxpayer theft
Trump seeks $152M to rebuild Alcatraz as ‘state-of-the-art secure prison’
Trump issues order attempting to change rules for mail-in voting
Democrats fume at White House budget request: ‘Bleak and unacceptable’

us-politics.headline: FBI Data Breach, Mosque Arrest, and Mace's Epstein Push
us-politics.body:
The FBI notified Congress of a "major incident" data breach affecting a surveillance system, offering few public details about the scope or origin of the intrusion. Immigration officers detained the president of a Milwaukee mosque, adding to ongoing tensions over ICE enforcement. Illinois Governor Pritzker called for Trump officials to testify about the crackdown; the White House dismissed it as a political stunt.

Rep. Nancy Mace said she is "not backing away" from demanding Attorney General Pam Bondi testify on the Epstein files. A federal case against a pro-life activist that began under the Harris Justice Department had its final charge dropped. A new survey finds roughly one in four Americans hold negative views of both major parties.

sources.title: Mace ‘not backing away’ from Bondi testifying on Epstein
Final charge dropped in yearslong Harris-era case against pro-life activist
Pritzker calls on Trump officials to testify over ICE crackdown, White House blasts move as ‘political stunt’
FBI labels data breach ‘major incident,’ notifies Congress
Immigration officers detain Wisconsin mosque president
About 1 in 4 hold negative views of both parties: Survey

au-politics.headline: Iran War Ripples Hit Gulf Shipping, Philippines Economy
au-politics.body:
The US-Iran conflict is spreading economic pain well beyond the battlefield. Around 20,000 sailors remain stranded on supertankers in the Gulf as the Strait of Hormuz becomes too dangerous to transit, threatening global oil supply chains. Iran's downing of a US jet has sharpened the crisis, with analysts warning it marks a potential quagmire moment for the Trump administration.

The Philippines is bearing some of the sharpest regional costs, with disrupted gas supplies pushing the country toward economic crisis. Australia's Energy Minister Chris Bowen has also addressed the fuel crisis domestically, as long-term caravanners report changing travel plans due to surging costs at the bowser.

sources.title: Iran's downing of a US jet will shock Americans in three ways
The 20,000 sailors who have become sitting ducks in the Gulf
How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis
Energy minister Chris Bowen on the impact of the fuel crisis - podcast
Another 'lockdown': How long-term caravanners are dealing with the fuel crisis

au-politics.headline: SA Liberals Cling to Opposition After One Nation Surge
au-politics.body:
South Australia's Liberal Party is fighting to retain official opposition status after One Nation's strong showing left the parliamentary numbers in doubt. The situation has few modern precedents in Australian state politics, raising questions about how the right-of-centre vote is fragmenting and whether the Liberals can hold their position without a formal arrangement with minor parties.

The result adds pressure to a federal political landscape already under strain. A Guardian analysis finds both Anthony Albanese and opposition treasury spokesman Angus Taylor facing voter demands on tax policy, with even some Coalition figures signalling miners could pay more. The election-year squeeze on both major parties is tightening.

sources.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status
Taxing times for Albanese and Taylor as parties seek to match voter expectations

au-politics.headline: Nasal Flu Spray Approved, but Free Access Remains Uneven
au-politics.body:
Australia's Therapeutic Goods Administration has approved a nasal spray flu vaccine, offering a needle-free option that is likely to appeal to children and needle-averse adults. The catch is that free access under the National Immunisation Program is not universal - eligibility depends on age and health status, leaving some families to pay out of pocket.

Health advocates say the gap in coverage risks undermining uptake at the population level. The rollout comes as the federal election campaign intensifies scrutiny of health spending commitments from both Labor and the Coalition.

sources.title: Nasal spray flu vaccines are here — but not all kids can get it for free

au-politics.headline: EV Novated Leases Grow, Telstra Hikes Sting Consumers
au-politics.body:
More Australians are using novated leases to access electric vehicles at a lower after-tax cost, exploiting a fringe benefits tax exemption that effectively subsidises the switch. The arrangement can save thousands annually, but experts warn of traps including residual value risk and obligations that persist if employment changes.

Separately, Telstra is drawing criticism for price rises on its starter mobile plan, with consumer advocates calling the increases unreasonable. Both stories point to the same pressure: households are actively hunting for ways to cut costs as living expenses remain elevated heading into the federal election.

sources.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV, but there are traps
Wake-up call: how Telstra’s ‘unreasonable’ price rises may cause customers to hang up

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Jets Downed, Hormuz Reopens
world.body:
Iran claims it shot down two US fighter jets, with search operations underway for at least one pilot still missing. The strikes follow a broader US-Israeli military campaign that has hit Iranian infrastructure, including a bridge attack survivors described as devastating, and damaged a factory in Israel from Iranian missile debris. The WHO reports more than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1.

Despite the fighting, shipping through the Strait of Hormuz resumed, with French, Omani, and Japanese vessels transiting the waterway for the first time since hostilities began. Trump, meanwhile, told reporters the US could eventually "take the oil" in Iran, while submitting a record $1.5 trillion defence budget request to Congress.

sources.title: Will force be used to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike
More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO
At least one killed at UAE’s Habshan gas facility after intercepted attack

What we know about the US fighter jet shot down in Iran

world.headline: Lebanon Christians Mark Good Friday Under Israeli Fire
world.body:
Christians in Lebanon observed Good Friday amid ongoing Israeli strikes, while monks held Easter prayers in a largely deserted Old City of Jerusalem. The juxtaposition of religious observance and active conflict underscored the grinding toll of the broader regional war on civilian life across the Middle East.

At least one person was killed after an intercepted attack on the UAE's Habshan gas facility, signalling that the conflict's reach is extending beyond the immediate frontlines into Gulf energy infrastructure.

sources.title: Christians in Lebanon observe Good Friday under Israeli attacks
Monks hold Easter prayers in deserted Old City of Jerusalem
At least one killed at UAE’s Habshan gas facility after intercepted attack

world.headline: Cuba Frees 2,000 Prisoners as US Pressure Bites
world.body:
Cuba has begun releasing more than 2,000 prisoners in a move widely seen as a response to sustained US diplomatic and economic pressure. The releases mark one of the largest such actions by Havana in recent years and suggest the government is seeking some form of relief from tightening sanctions.

The timing is notable. Cuba has faced intensifying economic hardship, and the prisoner releases appear calibrated to open space for negotiations rather than signal any broader political reform.

sources.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners amid mounting US pressure

world.headline: Democrat States Sue Trump Over Mail-In Ballot Crackdown
world.body:
More than two dozen Democrat-led states have filed a lawsuit challenging the Trump administration's restrictions on mail-in voting, escalating a legal battle over election access ahead of future federal contests. The states argue the limits are unconstitutional and disproportionately suppress voter participation.

Separately, a federal judge upheld the dismissal of subpoenas targeting Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, blocking what critics called an attempt to pressure the central bank. US unemployment figures dropped despite the economic turbulence generated by the Iran war and broader trade uncertainty.

sources.title: Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits
US judge upholds decision to toss subpoenas into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
US unemployment rate drops despite economic uncertainty and Iran war

ai.headline: OpenAI Reshuffles Leadership as Two Executives Step Back
ai.body:
OpenAI is reorganizing at the top, with COO Brad Lightcap moving into a new "special projects" role and consumer chief Fidji Simo taking medical leave. The company's AGI lead is also stepping away, leaving a notable gap at the center of its most ambitious research push.

The moves come as OpenAI faces mounting pressure to deliver on its AGI roadmap while managing rapid commercial expansion. Lightcap's new role suggests the company is carving out a separate track for high-priority initiatives outside normal operations. Three senior departures or reassignments in quick succession is not a routine shuffle.

sources.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up

ai.headline: Anthropic Buys Biotech, Launches PAC, Signals Broader Ambitions
ai.body:
Anthropic is spending aggressively beyond AI model development. The company reportedly acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, a bet that its AI systems can accelerate drug discovery and biological research. It also launched a political action committee, signaling it wants a direct hand in shaping AI regulation rather than lobbying from the sidelines.

The dual moves mark a sharp expansion in Anthropic's footprint. Buying a biotech firm puts it in direct competition with the life-sciences ambitions of OpenAI and Google DeepMind. The PAC puts it in Washington in a more formal, and more aggressive, way than before.

sources.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC

ai.headline: AI's Energy Bet on Gas Plants Draws Scrutiny
ai.body:
Microsoft, Meta, and Google are quietly funding the construction of large natural gas plants to power their AI data centers, bypassing slower renewable energy timelines in favor of guaranteed capacity. The strategy keeps training runs on schedule but locks in fossil fuel infrastructure for decades.

Meanwhile, Trump's push for a domestic AI data center buildout is stumbling - his own tariffs are raising construction costs and delaying hardware imports, undermining the policy goals his administration set. A separate survey found communities prefer Amazon warehouses to data centers, citing noise, water use, and minimal local job creation. The energy and siting politics around AI infrastructure are getting harder to ignore.

sources.title: Trump ignores biggest reasons his AI data center buildout is failing
AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center

ai.headline: Meta Data Breach, Musk's IPO Demands Rattle AI Business World
ai.body:
Meta has paused its relationship with AI hiring platform Mercor after a data breach exposed sensitive information about the company's AI projects. The incident highlights how third-party contractors have become a significant security vulnerability for major AI labs handling proprietary research.

Separately, Elon Musk is reportedly requiring banks involved in a potential SpaceX IPO to purchase Grok subscriptions, using deal access as leverage to drive revenue for his AI product. The tactic blurs the line between business negotiation and coercion. Researchers also published findings this week showing AI users increasingly defer to model outputs without critical evaluation - a pattern they call "cognitive surrender" - suggesting the industry's influence over human reasoning is growing faster than most anticipated.

sources.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
Elon Musk insists banks working on SpaceX IPO must buy Grok subscriptions
"Cognitive surrender" leads AI users to abandon logical thinking, research finds

btc.headline: Circle Faces Heat After $285M Drift Hack and Compliance Failures
btc.body:
Circle is under mounting pressure after the $285 million Drift protocol exploit, with critics accusing the stablecoin issuer of failing to freeze stolen USDC quickly enough. On-chain investigator ZachXBT went further, alleging Circle has racked up $420 million in compliance failures since 2022. The backlash raises hard questions about whether USDC's freeze mechanism - a key selling point for institutional users - is actually enforced in practice.

Drift sent an on-chain message to wallets linked to the hack, a tactic that signals limited options for recovery. The episode sharpens a broader debate about execution risk in crypto. Holding assets is no longer the main vulnerability - what happens when you try to move or protect them is.

sources.title: Circle under fire after $285 million Drift hack over inaction to freeze stolen USDC
ZachXBT accuses Circle of $420M in 'compliance failures' since 2022
Execution risk in crypto is the new custody risk
Drift sends onchain message to wallets tied to $280M exploit

btc.headline: Bitcoin Dips Below $70K as Corporate Treasury Strategies Diverge
btc.body:
Bitcoin slipped below $70,000 this week, and the pressure is exposing cracks in the corporate treasury playbook. Companies that followed MicroStrategy's accumulation model are now sitting on paper losses, while a rival camp - led by Nakamoto-style treasury vehicles - is taking a more conservative approach. The divergence signals that the institutional Bitcoin trade is maturing and fragmenting.

Macro headwinds are adding noise. A former hedge fund manager argues Bitcoin is underpricing the risk of a prolonged Iran conflict, while Cathie Wood insists the era of 85% drawdowns is over and set a new $34,000 floor target. Futures markets are tilting bearish, volatility is cooling, and altcoins are quietly outperforming BTC on the day.

sources.title: Is Bitcoin mispricing a prolonged Iran war? Ex-hedge fund manager weighs in
Crypto Biz: Bitcoin treasuries break ranks as BTC dips below $70K
Price predictions 4/3: BTC, ETH, BNB, XRP, SOL, DOGE, HYPE, ADA, BCH, LINK
CoinDesk 20 performance update: Bitcoin (BTC) trades flat while altcoins rise
Bitcoin 'done' with 85% crashes, says Cathie Wood amid new $34K target
Crypto consolidates as volatility cools and futures markets tilt bearish

btc.headline: Ethereum Foundation Hits 70,000 ETH Staking Target
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation completed its staking push, deploying another $93 million in ETH to reach its 70,000 ETH target. The move is a public commitment to network participation from an organization that has historically kept its ETH liquid. It also arrives as Offchain Labs argues that Ethereum Layer 2 networks need more responsive fee pricing to scale to billions of users.

Schwab added to the broader Ethereum narrative, announcing plans to launch spot Bitcoin and Ether trading in the first half of 2026. That would bring direct crypto exposure to one of the largest retail brokerage platforms in the US - a meaningful step beyond ETF access.

sources.title: Ethereum Foundation nearly reaches 70,000 staked ETH goal
Ethereum L2s need responsive pricing to scale, says Offchain Labs
Ethereum Foundation stakes another $93 million ether, reaching its 70,000 ETH target
Schwab plans spot bitcoin, ether trading launch in first half of 2026

btc.headline: Coinbase Trust Charter Draws Banking Opposition, Crypto Regulation Heats Up
btc.body:
US community banks are pushing back against the OCC's move to grant Coinbase a trust charter, arguing it gives the crypto exchange an unfair regulatory advantage. The opposition reflects a wider turf war as crypto firms seek bank-like powers without bank-like constraints. Meanwhile, Cambodia proposed serious prison sentences for crypto scammers, and Malta is clashing with the EU's ESMA over who controls crypto oversight in smaller member states.

The regulatory front is busy on multiple continents. Each battle - whether over charters, criminal penalties, or supranational authority - is shaping the rules that will govern the next phase of crypto adoption.

sources.title: US community banks oppose OCC's approval of Coinbase trust charter
Cambodian lawmakers propose severe prison time for crypto scammers
Centralizing crypto: Why Malta’s clash with ESMA is about more than one small state

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust you'll get under the broiler

exercise.name: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy dumbbells, open floor space
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer's carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries at near-maximal load with short rest periods — brutally effective for grip, core stability, and real-world strength that most gym sessions ignore

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — and when applied to humans, whose cells, memories, and beliefs constantly change, it becomes a genuine crisis of what 'you' even means across time.
learn.why: It's a Friday, which makes it the perfect day to spend an hour with Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' — his thought experiments on teleportation and fission will quietly unsettle assumptions you didn't know you were carrying
